text:
Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily who loved to draw. Every weekend, she would race scribble-pencil drawings across big sheets.

Her favorite topic? Animals. She drew squirrels clambering up cork-oak trees, butterflies humming around blossoms, and lions chasing after wildebeests.

One hot summer day, her aunt told her they were leaving for Morocco. Worried about her favorite furry friend, Lily wrote letters to them. She drew sketches of Moony and made notes.

Months went by, and there was a heavy fog. Lily worried Moony hadn’t come home. She drew pictures of foxes and owl nests until daylight arrived.

Her owl owl flew her deep into the woods. Surprise! There he was! Dreamy moors with big stone towers.

Lily chased Moony along pathways. They hunted for snails in shells. When she felt silly, she drew silly giggling giraffes.

She found tiny lanterns. They flick
